differential_diagnoses: Dilated cardiomyopathy secondary to chronic alcohol use
Ischemic cardiomyopathy (due to prior microvascular or embolic events)
next_steps: Serologic testing for Trypanosoma cruzi antibodies
Genetic counseling and comprehensive cardiomyopathy gene panel testing
Implantable cardioverter-defibrillator (ICD) implantation for primary prevention
Holter monitoring for extended assessment of arrhythmia burden
Evaluation for cardiac transplantation or advanced heart failure therapies if functional status fails to improve
Strict adherence to guideline-directed medical therapy (GDMT)
Perform serologic testing for Trypanosoma cruzi (ELISA and confirmatory IFA)
Refer for ICD implantation as per current GDMT guidelines
Order cardiac genetic counseling and cardiomyopathy gene panel testing
Infectious disease consultation to discuss potential anti-parasitic treatment if Chagas is confirmed
Continue longitudinal outpatient monitoring with telemetry/Holter to evaluate arrhythmia burden
Evaluate for advanced heart failure therapies if ejection fraction does not improve
